items.effect: rs4988235: C/T associated with lactase persistence (lactose tolerance). T allele is the derived allele conferring continued lactase production into adulthood.
items.caveat: Research-only trait association. Population-specific: T allele common in European populations, rare in East Asian populations.

items.effect: rs1801133: C677T variant reduces MTHFR enzyme activity by ~35% per T allele. Homozygous TT (677TT) has ~70% reduced activity, associated with elevated homocysteine levels.
items.caveat: Research-only. ~10-15% of Europeans are TT homozygous. Clinical significance debated; folate supplementation may mitigate risk.

items.effect: rs429358: C allele in combination with rs7412 defines APOE isoform. APOE ε4 (defined by rs429358-C, rs7412-C) is the strongest common genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease.
items.source: Corder et al. 1993, Science; Lambert et al. 2013, Nature Genetics
items.caveat: Research-only. APOE ε4 is a risk factor, not deterministic. Many ε4 carriers never develop AD. Interpret with rs7412 for full isoform determination.

items.effect: rs7412: T allele combined with rs429358 genotype defines APOE isoform. T at rs7412 + C at rs429358 = APOE ε3. Both reference = APOE ε4.
items.effect_direction: T at rs7412 = ε3 or ε2 allele component
items.caveat: Research-only. Must be interpreted together with rs429358 for full APOE isoform: ε2=(T,T), ε3=(T,C), ε4=(C,C) at rs7412,rs429358.

items.effect: rs1815739: C/T (R577X). T allele creates a stop codon (X allele), resulting in absence of α-actinin-3 protein in fast-twitch muscle fibers. Homozygous XX (TT) is overrepresented in endurance athletes.
items.effect_direction: T (X allele) = endurance orientation; C (R allele) = power/sprint orientation
items.source: Yang et al. 2003, American Journal of Human Genetics
items.caveat: Research-only. ~18% of world population is XX homozygous. Athletic performance is highly polygenic; ACTN3 is one small contributor.

items.effect: rs671: G/A (E504K, also called ALDH2*2). A allele dramatically reduces aldehyde dehydrogenase 2 activity, causing alcohol flush reaction ('Asian flush') and acetaldehyde accumulation.
items.effect_direction: A = alcohol flush, reduced alcohol metabolism
items.source: Crabb et al. 1989, Journal of Clinical Investigation
items.caveat: Research-only. ~30-40% of East Asian populations carry the A allele. Strongly associated with increased esophageal cancer risk with alcohol consumption.

items.effect: rs1800562: C282Y variant in HFE gene. Homozygous C282Y is the most common cause of hereditary hemochromatosis, leading to excessive iron absorption and iron overload.
items.effect_direction: A (Y allele) = increased iron overload risk when homozygous
items.caveat: Research-only. ~1 in 200 Europeans are C282Y homozygous. Penetrance is variable; not all homozygotes develop clinical disease. Compound heterozygosity with H63D also relevant.

items.effect: rs1799945: H63D variant in HFE gene. H63D homozygosity or compound heterozygosity with C282Y may cause mild iron overload. Less severe than C282Y homozygosity.
items.effect_direction: G (D allele) = mild iron overload risk
items.caveat: Research-only. H63D is common (~15% carrier frequency in Europeans). Clinical significance as homozygous or compound het is debated.

items.effect: rs9939609: T/A variant in FTO intron. A allele is associated with increased BMI (~0.35 kg/m² per allele) and obesity risk through appetite regulation effects.
items.caveat: Research-only. FTO has the largest effect size among common obesity variants but accounts for <1% of BMI variance. Environmental factors dominate.

items.effect: rs6265: G/A (Val66Met). A allele (Met) reduces activity-dependent BDNF secretion. Associated with differences in episodic memory, hippocampal volume, and susceptibility to psychiatric conditions.
items.caveat: Research-only. ~20-30% of populations carry at least one Met allele. Effect sizes for cognitive/psychiatric phenotypes are small and context-dependent.

items.effect: rs4680: G/A (Val158Met). A allele (Met) reduces COMT enzyme activity by ~3-4x, leading to higher prefrontal dopamine levels. Associated with differences in stress response, pain sensitivity, and cognitive function.
items.effect_direction: A (Met) = higher dopamine, 'warrior' vs 'worrier' personality archetype
items.caveat: Research-only. COMT Val/Met has been extensively studied but effect sizes for behavioral phenotypes are small. The 'warrior/worrier' framing is oversimplified.
